summary: Stellar Passenger, Scarlet>Fire and jam‑heavy second set
blurb: The show shines with a soaring Passenger and a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition, while the second‑set jams on Playing In The Band, Not Fade Away and Wharf Rat are standout moments. Jerry recovers from early guitar issues to deliver inventive solos, and Brent’s electric piano adds spacey texture.
review: On March 5, 1981 at Pittsburgh’s Stanley Theatre the Dead delivered a high‑energy outing that has earned a reputation as a "killer" show among fans. After an early hiccup with Jerry’s guitar in set 2, he bursts back on Passenger, launching a soaring second half that rolls straight into a flawless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue. From there the band stretches out on Playing In The Band, letting Brent’s electric piano float over a thick wall of drums and bass before diving into an expansive Space jam that feeds into a punchy Not Fade Away and a soulful Wharf Rat. Highlights from set 1 include The Music Never Stopped and To Lay Me Down, while the encore U.S. Blues caps the night on a high note. Across multiple recordings fans praise Jerry’s inventive solos, Phil’s bass “bombs,” Bob’s slide work, and Brent’s spacey keyboards, making this performance a must‑listen for anyone chasing that early‑’80s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five recordings, with consistent praise for Passenger, Scarlet>Fire, Playing In The Band and overall "killer" show description; no recurring negative comments about performance.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape captures the electrifying energy of the night, includes all standout songs and offers a clear, listenable experience despite occasional crowd noise.

key_highlights: Passenger launches the second set after early guitar hiccups, soaring high.
Seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition fuels an explosive jam.
Playing In The Band expands into a spacey, keyboard‑driven journey with Brent shining.
Not Fade Away and Wharf Rat close the night with punchy, rhythmic drive.

band_performance.Jerry: After a brief guitar wobble at the start of set 2, Jerry launches into soaring leads on Passenger and To Lay Me Down, delivering inventive solos throughout, especially the transition into Fire.
band_performance.Phil: Phil drops a few bass bombs and thunders through the tight groove, providing a solid foundation that anchors the extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Bob steps in with slide work on Passenger, takes lead vocals when Jerry is out, and keeps steady rhythm across both sets.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s laid‑back electric piano adds spacey texture, shines on the Playin’ jam and drops tasty licks on Friend Of The Devil, though his tone gets buried after he switches instruments.
band_performance.Billy: Bill’s drum break offers a refreshing breather before Space, then joins Mickey in an intricate, mind‑blowing drum improvisation that drives the second‑set flow.
